Preconditions: Application starts from Main.java and opens the Swing UI.
Test Data: None.
Test Steps: 1. Launch the program. 2. Observe the login screen before interacting with it.
Expected Result: The login title, email field, password field, and buttons are visible, aligned, readable, and not clipped.

Preconditions: Log in as an HR employee assigned to division 3.
Test Data: Valid HR email and last 4 SSN digits.
Test Steps: 1. Log in as HR. 2. Observe the HR Admin Menu screen.
Expected Result: The HR menu clearly shows Employee Management, Payroll / Salary Management, Reports, and Logout in an organized layout.

Preconditions: Log in as a non-HR employee.
Test Data: Valid employee email and last 4 SSN digits.
Test Steps: 1. Log in as a general employee. 2. Observe the Employee Menu screen.
Expected Result: The employee menu shows only Search / View My Profile, View My Pay Statement History, and Logout. No HR-only controls are visible.

Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Any searchable employee record.
Test Steps: 1. Open the shared employee search screen. 2. Enter a search and run it. 3. Inspect the result table.
Expected Result: The search type control, search field, buttons, and result table are visible. Table columns are readable and can be scrolled if they do not fit.

Preconditions: A search result exists and View Details opens successfully.
Test Data: Any employee record.
Test Steps: 1. Open Employee Details from a search result. 2. Scroll through the details screen.
Expected Result: The details screen is readable and scrollable. No content is cut off at the bottom.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: None.
Test Steps: 1. Open Add New Employee. 2. Inspect all labels, fields, and action buttons.
Expected Result: All fields are aligned and readable. Helper notes are visible. Buttons do not overlap the form.
Cleanup / Reset: Return to the HR Employee Management menu.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: None.
Test Steps: 1. Open Update Salaries by Percentage and Range. 2. Inspect fields and result area.
Expected Result: Minimum salary, maximum salary, percentage, and result area are visible and readable. Buttons are aligned and not clipped.
Cleanup / Reset: Return to the HR Payroll menu.

Preconditions: Payroll history exists for the selected employee.
Test Data: Any employee with payroll rows.
Test Steps: 1. Open Payroll History. 2. Inspect the table and labels.
Expected Result: Payroll columns are readable, rows are visible, and the table can be scrolled horizontally if needed.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: None.
Test Steps: 1. Open Reports. 2. Switch between Job Title, Division, and New Hires report types.
Expected Result: The correct input rows appear for each report type. Hidden rows stay hidden and the table remains readable.

Preconditions: Reach any screen that shows validation or confirmation dialogs.
Test Data: Use invalid input or delete confirmation.
Test Steps: 1. Trigger an error dialog. 2. Trigger a confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: Dialog text is readable, fully visible, and clear about what happened.
Cleanup / Reset: Dismiss dialogs and return to the prior screen.

Preconditions: Application is open on the login screen.
Test Data: Invalid email and any 4-digit password.
Test Steps: 1. Enter a non-existent email. 2. Enter any 4-digit password. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Login fails. An error dialog appears. The app remains on the login screen and the password field is cleared.

Preconditions: A valid non-HR employee exists in the database.
Test Data: Employee email and last 4 digits of SSN.
Test Steps: 1. Enter the employee email. 2. Enter the last 4 digits of the employee SSN. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Login succeeds and the Employee Menu appears.

Preconditions: A valid HR employee exists in division 3.
Test Data: HR email and last 4 digits of SSN.
Test Steps: 1. Enter the HR employee email. 2. Enter the last 4 digits of the HR employee SSN. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Login succeeds and the HR Admin Menu appears.

Preconditions: A user is currently logged in.
Test Data: Any valid account.
Test Steps: 1. Click Logout. 2. Observe the screen. 3. Log in as a different user.
Expected Result: The session is cleared, the login screen is shown, and the next login uses the new account without showing the old user's data.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee.
Test Data: The logged-in employee's own empID.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search / View My Profile. 2. Search by Employee ID using the logged-in employee's own empID.
Expected Result: The employee's own record is returned and can be opened with View Details.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee.
Test Data: The logged-in employee's own name.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search / View My Profile. 2. Search by Name using the logged-in employee's own first name, last name, or full name.
Expected Result: Only the logged-in employee's own record is returned.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee and the employee has a DOB record in addresses.
Test Data: The logged-in employee's own DOB in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search / View My Profile. 2. Search by DOB using the logged-in employee's DOB.
Expected Result: Only the logged-in employee's own record is returned.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee and another employee exists.
Test Data: Another employee's empID, name, DOB, or SSN.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search / View My Profile. 2. Search using another employee's information.
Expected Result: The system blocks access. It shows an access-denied message or no permitted result. The other employee's record is not shown.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee and the employee found their own record.
Test Data: The logged-in employee's own record.
Test Steps: 1. Search for the logged-in employee. 2. Open View Details.
Expected Result: Employee details are visible in read-only mode. HR-only Save / Delete behavior is not available.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee with payroll rows.
Test Data: The logged-in employee account.
Test Steps: 1. Open View My Pay Statement History.
Expected Result: Only the logged-in employee's payroll history is shown, sorted by most recent pay date first.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: A known employee empID.
Test Steps: 1. Open Employee Management > Search Employee. 2. Search by Employee ID.
Expected Result: The matching employee appears in the results table.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: A partial first name or last name that exists.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search Employee. 2. Search by Name.
Expected Result: Matching employees appear in the results table.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and at least one employee has a DOB record.
Test Data: A DOB in YYYY-MM-DD format that exists.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search Employee. 2. Search by DOB.
Expected Result: Matching employees appear in the results table.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: A known employee SSN.
Test Steps: 1. Open Search Employee. 2. Search by SSN.
Expected Result: The matching employee appears in the results table.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and a target employee exists.
Test Data: Use a dedicated test employee if possible.
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Open Edit Selected. 3. Change one editable field such as email. 4. Save.
Expected Result: The save succeeds, a success message appears, and searching again shows the updated value.
Cleanup / Reset: Restore the original field value after the test.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR. A valid addressID already exists. A valid division ID already exists.
Test Data: Use a unique email and SSN for the new test employee.
Test Steps: 1. Open Add New Employee. 2. Enter valid data in all fields. 3. Click Add Employee.
Expected Result: A success message appears and the new employee can be found by search.
Cleanup / Reset: Delete the created test employee after verification.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: Use a valid form except for an invalid hire date.
Test Steps: 1. Open Add New Employee. 2. Enter an invalid hire date such as 15-06-2024. 3. Click Add Employee.
Expected Result: The add is blocked and an invalid-date message appears. No new employee is inserted.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and a target employee exists.
Test Data: Use a dedicated test employee if possible.
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Click Delete Selected. 3. Choose No in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: The employee is not deleted.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and a dedicated test employee exists.
Test Data: Create a disposable employee first if needed.
Test Steps: 1. Search for the disposable employee. 2. Click Delete Selected. 3. Choose Yes in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: The employee is deleted and no longer appears in search results.
Cleanup / Reset: None if the deleted employee was created only for this test.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and at least one employee exists in the chosen salary range.
Test Data: A salary range that contains known employees and a positive percentage such as 5.
Test Steps: 1. Open Payroll / Salary Management > Update Salaries by Percentage and Range. 2. Enter min salary, max salary, and percentage. 3. Click Apply Update. 4. Confirm.
Expected Result: The update completes and the result area reports the number of affected employee records.
Cleanup / Reset: Record original salaries first, then restore them manually after the test.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: Valid min/max salary values and a negative percentage.
Test Steps: 1. Open the salary update screen. 2. Enter a negative percentage. 3. Click Apply Update.
Expected Result: The update is blocked and an error dialog explains that the percentage must be positive.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: Non-numeric text in one numeric field.
Test Steps: 1. Open the salary update screen. 2. Enter letters in one numeric field. 3. Click Apply Update.
Expected Result: The update is blocked and an error dialog explains that valid numbers are required.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and a target employee with payroll exists.
Test Data: A target employee with pay history.
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Choose View Pay History.
Expected Result: The selected employee's payroll history appears, newest first.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and payroll/job title data exists for the selected month.
Test Data: A valid month and year.
Test Steps: 1. Open Reports > Total Pay by Job Title for Month. 2. Enter month and year. 3. Run the report.
Expected Result: The report shows job titles with total pay for the selected month.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and payroll/division data exists for the selected month.
Test Data: A valid month and year.
Test Steps: 1. Open Reports > Total Pay by Division for Month. 2. Enter month and year. 3. Run the report.
Expected Result: The report shows divisions with total pay for the selected month.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR and employees exist in the selected hire-date range.
Test Data: A valid start date and end date.
Test Steps: 1. Open Reports > New Hires by Date Range. 2. Enter start date and end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. 3. Run the report.
Expected Result: The report lists employees hired within the date range.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee.
Test Data: None.
Test Steps: 1. Inspect the Employee Menu. 2. Attempt to reach Reports, Add Employee, Update Employee, Delete Employee, or Salary Update through normal UI navigation.
Expected Result: HR-only functions are not shown on the employee menu and cannot be opened through normal UI actions.

Preconditions: Logged in as a non-HR employee and another employee exists.
Test Data: Another employee's empID.
Test Steps: 1. Try to open payroll history for another employee through any available path.
Expected Result: The system blocks access and shows an access-denied message or returns to the Employee Menu.

Preconditions: Logged in as HR.
Test Data: Invalid report dates or non-numeric month/year.
Test Steps: 1. Open a report screen. 2. Enter invalid month/year or invalid date format. 3. Run the report.
Expected Result: The report does not run. The app shows a clear validation message and stays stable.

Preconditions: Both an HR account and a non-HR account are available.
Test Data: Valid login credentials for both roles.
Test Steps: 1. Log in as HR and navigate through each HR submenu. 2. Log out. 3. Log in as a general employee and navigate through the employee menu. 4. Log out.
Expected Result: Navigation stays consistent, back buttons return to the correct menu, and role-specific screens appear only for the correct user.
